title: Zimbra urges customers to patch critical web client XSS flaw
summary: The Zimbra security team urged customers to patch a critical vulnerability affecting the Classic Web Client used to access the Zimbra Collaboration suite. [...]

title: Former ransomware negotiator gets 4 years for BlackCat attacks
summary: A former employee of cybersecurity incident response company DigitalMint was sentenced to 70 months in prison for targeting U.S. companies in BlackCat (ALPHV) ransomware attacks. [...]

title: OpenMandriva Linux says contributor tried to sabotage the project
summary: The OpenMandriva Linux project announced that it was the target of an attempted act of internal sabotage after a dispute among contributors. [...]

title: Injective SDK on npm infected with cryptocurrency wallet stealer
summary: Hackers compromised the Injective Labs SDK project's GitHub repository and used it to publish a malicious package on the Node Package Manager (npm) that stole cryptocurrency wallet private keys and mnemonic seed phrases. [...]

title: New Helix vishing group emerges in SharePoint data theft attacks
summary: A new data-extortion group called Helix is using identity-focused tactics such as voice phishing (vishing), device code phishing, and multi-factor authentication (MFA) abuse to steal data from SharePoint environments. [...]

title: Microsoft expects more Windows security updates from AI-discovered flaws
summary: Microsoft says Windows users should expect to see an increase in security updates as the company increasingly relies on artificial intelligence to discover vulnerabilities in its codebase. [...]

title: New Forg365 phishing platform uses AI to target Microsoft 365 accounts
summary: A new phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) operation called Forg365 focuses on stealing Microsoft 365 accounts by combining adversary-in-the-middle (AiTM) and device code methods with AI-assisted lure generation. [...]

title: The Hidden Security Risks of Reduced Summer IT Coverage
summary: Security operations don't slow down when IT teams take vacation, but staffing levels often do. Kaseya explains how AI-driven automation can help organizations maintain consistent security operations and reduce reliance on manual processes year-round. [...]

title: Microsoft to retire the OWA Light client in Exchange Server
summary: Microsoft has announced plans to disable Outlook Web Access (OWA) Light, the lightweight version of the Outlook Web App email client, in a future Exchange Server update. [...]

title: Police arrests 5,800 suspects in global anti-fraud crackdown
summary: Law enforcement agencies have arrested 5,811 suspects and seized $293 million in illicit assets in a global anti-fraud operation spanning 97 countries. [...]

title: AssuranceAmerica data breach exposes records of 6.9 million drivers
summary: American insurance company AssuranceAmerica has disclosed a data breach impacting nearly 7 million drivers after attackers gained access to its systems earlier this year.  [...]

title: Microsoft patches RoguePlanet Defender zero-day vulnerability
summary: Microsoft has released a security patch to address a Defender zero-day vulnerability known as "RoguePlanet," disclosed after the June 2026 Patch Tuesday. [...]

title: Mount Royal University confirms breach as hackers claim attack
summary: Mount Royal University in Calgary says hackers stole and then deleted data from its file storage systems after breaching the university's network. [...]

title: Fake Paysafe, Skrill SDKs on NPM and PyPi steal credentials
summary: Malicious packages on the Node Package Manager (npm) and the Python Package Index (PyPI) delivered stealer malware to developers and users of Paysafe, Skrill, and Neteller payment applications. [...]

title: Hackers exploit Roundcube flaw to spy on academic researchers
summary: A China-linked threat cluster has been exploiting vulnerable Roundcube servers at U.S. and Canadian universities to steal credentials and deploy backdoor malware. [...]
